head: His forehead up against the glass
subhead: Stice stuck to window; Hal’s tic; Kenkle & Brandt
capsule: Hal brushes his teeth, finds the window open in the boys bathroom. Subsequently comes across Stice with his forehead frozen to a window in the dorm’s hallway. He asks Troeltsch to watch his toothbrush (“only the worst kind of naïf leaves his toothbrush unattended around ETA…”) and goes to get help. Hal’s uncontrolled ticks start to manifest themselves. He speaks with the ETA janitorial staff and the ticks continue.

head: Stuff of fucking nightmares
capsule: “Weather-delayed meeting” between Rodney Tine, Sr. (and staff) with Glad regarding Year of Glad and Fully Functional Phil, the prancing ass.

head: Let them come as they will, but do not entertain them

head: Real beaks and claws, intense horizontality

head: Out of nowhere, the sidewalk comes rushing up to meet you

head: Desiccated, deprived of some essential fluid
subhead: Pemulis wants to interface with an unimpressed Hal
capsule: Hal talks with Pemulis, who is concerned that the DMZ is gone, then watches part of his father’s film ‘Good-Looking Men in Small Clever Rooms That Utilize Every Centimeter of Available Space with Mind-Boggling Efficiency’.

head: Real addiction and what it can turn someone into
capsule: More of Gately’s history working for Whitey Sorkin with Gene Fackelmann. Gately watches Orin, “The B.U. punter,” on TV “just after Xmas then has a breakdown over his wasted athletic talent.

head: Looking up into the darkness
capsule: Pemulis finds several drop-ceiling panels on the floor of Subdormitory B. He’s looking for the DMZ in an old sneaker behind the relevant panel. (Makes sense that Pemulis would go to gather his stash from its hiding spot after he was kicked out. As for who took it – that’s one of the big questions of IJ. A popular theory for Hal’s strange illness at the end of the book is that he was dosed with the DMZ. So who would swipe the DMZ and dose Hal? The wraith trying to bring Hal out of his shell perhaps? The AFR spy John Wayne? Avril? An infiltrating AFR agent arriving in advance of the rest of the group in the tennis bus?)

head: A thing that basically hides
subhead: Gately: Fackleman’s HUGE windfall; digging dream.
capsule: More on Gene Fackelmann and Gately’s ex-girlfriend Pamela Hoffman-Jeep. Back in the hospital, Gately dreams about riding in “a bus the same color as its own exhaust” aka the Paragon bus gray line of the next scene. Lyle in wraith form licks Gately’s forehead. Gately has a prescient dream of Hal and Joelle in a graveyard digging up James Incandenza’s head.
themes: Recur, Cycles, Inf/Reg, Waste, His/Father, Isolation, Memory, Non-Action, Time, Dreams

head: Footnote 110
subhead: Hal & Orin discuss Quebec (Still separatist? Or Anti-ONAN?) plus a sampling of Avril’s correspondance with Orin
capsule:
“here later in the same day, 11/7” Near 1600h EST (Page 1009: “What is it out there, 1600h?”), 1400h in Arizona. Orin and Hal talk on the phone about Steeply, ONAN politics, the samizdat. (Read around the letters from and to the Moms. Orin is still considering Helen Steeply the “Subject,” not Luria. Steeply has yet to be dropped off at the airport.)
Avril writes to Orin in Arizona, but receives a form letter in reply. (These letters are wedged in the middle of Footnote 110. Read only the letters.)
plotlines: E.T.A., Hal, A.F.R., Mario, Orin, Avril, O.N.A.N.
themes: Recur, Cycles, Secrets, Waste, His/Father, Choice

head: Footnote 145
capsule: Transcript of Steeply’s soft profile on Orin. (This is dated Nov. 3, the same day Orin calls Hal to ask about Canadian Separatism (see section 135-137). Orin calls because the interview has put him on his toes, so it’s logical to have the chronological reader read the interview before coming to the phone call, which presumably takes place in the evening after the long day at ETA.)
